summary_one_line: Input must carry a chunk whose 4-byte FOURCC name field equals png_tRNS to take the true branch.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A 4-byte chunk-name field somewhere in the PNG chunk stream that exactly equals the tRNS FOURCC, so that the equality test 'chunk_name == png_tRNS' at pngread.c:917 evaluates true and png_handle_tRNS is reached.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: All five cmplog winner seeds converge on one concentrated chunk-header byte configuration (offsets 0x13-0x1e identical across all 8 winners), and the per-role overlay shows the winner reaches line 918 (png_handle_tRNS) which only fires when the 4-byte chunk_name matches the tRNS literal exactly. The cmplog CMP buffer logged the 'chunk_name == png_tRNS' comparison and I2SRandReplace spliced the matching operand into the chunk-name bytes.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The ten naive loser seeds never resolve the branch (T=0 at line 917 in L) and instead exit through other chunk-name arms or the default; their chunk-header bytes are scattered (offsets 0x13-0x1e show many distinct values, e.g. 0x13 has 05/ab/5b/04 and 0x1d has 00/7f/52/ff), so they never place the exact tRNS FOURCC and fall through the if-else chain.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog intercepts the 4-byte integer compare 'chunk_name == png_tRNS' via __sanitizer_cov_trace_cmp4, records both operands in the CmpLogObserver buffer, and I2SRandReplace then scans the input and substitutes the tRNS operand at the chunk-name offset. naive has no CMP introspection and must hit the exact 4-byte magic by chance, which it does not in 10 trials.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is an equality test of chunk_name against the tRNS FOURCC literal.
Taking the true branch dispatches into png_handle_tRNS, reached only in the winner.
cmplog resolved the branch in all 10 trials while naive resolved 0/10.
The naive loser never takes the tRNS true side; the divergent-branch table shows L T=0 at line 917.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: T=14 F=7  T=0 F=54  else if (chunk_name == png_tRNS)  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Winner chunk-header bytes are concentrated to a single configuration while loser bytes are scattered across many values.
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace, which substitutes a logged CMP operand at matching input offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive has no I2S mutator and must hit the magic by chance.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 3902 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 1958fbcf6f72d79d`; the ancestor chain has 2 I2S-floor dash rows (mutation_op = -) at depths 2 and 3, the tool's lower-bound signal of an unwrapped I2SRandReplace find under the current cmplog build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: If a synthetic harness with a 4-byte exact-equality FOURCC gate is resolved by naive as often as by cmplog within the same trial budget, the I2S magic-substitution attribution is refuted.

weakest_evidence_point: The byte-diff is reported at fixed early offsets (chunk-header region) rather than at the actual variable offset of the tRNS chunk-name in each seed, so the exact location of the matched FOURCC is inferred, not pinpointed.
